lead.headline: Rockies sneak past Angels in one-run thriller
lead.whatHappened: Colorado walked out of Anaheim with a 9-8 win after the Angels couldn't close it out. The kind of game where both teams had chances to put it away and neither did.
lead.whyBullet1: The Rockies are scrappy enough to steal games on the road when better teams get sloppy.
lead.whyBullet2: The Angels keep finding ways to lose close ones — that's a pattern, not bad luck.
lead.whatToSay: The Angels had them. They just couldn't finish.

sportsOther: Tigers barely held off Tampa in a sloppy one.
Brewers absolutely demolished San Francisco—16 runs is just embarrassing.
Rockies sneaked past LA in what should've been Angels' game.

markets.mood: Tech had a split personality today—chip stocks and Tesla climbed while Microsoft got hammered 3.9%, leaving the broader market basically flat with SPY up just 0.2%.
markets.whyBullet1: If your 401k is heavy on Microsoft or Dell (down 4.4%), today stung; if you're long NVIDIA or Apple, you made ground back.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch Thursday and Friday for earnings whispers and any Fed commentary—Microsoft's drop suggests people are spooked about something specific in their guidance, and that fear can spread fast.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin tanked 5.1% while stocks mostly held. That used to mean risk-off everywhere. Now they're decoupled. Your crypto buddy and your boomer uncle are in completely different markets.

glance.sports: Tigers edged Rays 10-9 in a tight one; Brewers demolished Giants 16-2.
glance.market: Tech mixed: NVDA and TSLA both up over 1%, but MSFT dropped 3.9% and DELL fell 4.4%; SPY +0.2%.
glance.bestConvo: That Brewers-Giants game wasn't a baseball game, it was a crime scene — 16-2 is the kind of score that gets you fired.
glance.watchNext: Knicks-Spurs NBA Finals Game 1 tomorrow night in San Antonio; Monaco Grand Prix this weekend; World Cup qualifying opens June 11.
glance.quickRec: Microsoft's down 3.9% today — worth asking why before you panic or buy the dip.
